meta_title: For Freelancers: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no testing knowledge (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no testing knowledge. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Claude Code Skills Setup: Testing for Freelance Developers
content.seo_description: Stop fearing bugs. Learn to leverage Claude Code and Antigravity to automate testing and deliver rock-solid code for your clients in record time.
content.answer_block: Eliminate testing anxiety by integrating Claude Code as your autonomous QA engineer. By configuring Antigravity to map your codebase, you can generate unit tests automatically. This setup allows you to ship features with high confidence, ensuring client deliverables are bug-free while reducing your manual development time by 40%.
content.intro:
The clock is ticking, the client is waiting, and you’re staring at a codebase that feels like a house of cards. Every feature you push feels like a gamble because you lack a formal testing suite. You aren't a QA engineer, and you don't have time to write thousands of lines of boilerplate test scripts. The fear of breaking production isn't just a technical hurdle—it's a bottleneck to your scaling as a freelancer.

But what if your code could test itself? By combining the architectural oversight of Antigravity with the execution power of Claude Code, you transform from a solo coder into a one-person engineering team. This workflow isn't just about finding bugs; it’s about building a bulletproof reputation that justifies your premium rates.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Context
steps.description: Before Claude can test your code, it needs a mental map. Use Antigravity to index your project structure and identify high-risk modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the project directory and generate a dependency graph. Identify the top 3 most critical modules that require unit test coverage for production stability.

steps.title: Set the Testing Standard
steps.description: Define the testing framework constraints so Claude produces code that fits your existing environment perfectly.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a testing standards document for this project. Define the use of Jest, mocking strategies for API calls, and naming conventions for test suites.

steps.title: Generate Baseline Test Suites
steps.description: Use Claude Code to ingest the dependency map and generate initial test files for your core logic.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Using the dependency map, generate unit tests for the identified critical modules. Ensure 80% branch coverage.
steps.claude_code_command: claude "generate unit tests for ./src/services/auth.ts based on the standards in standards.md"

steps.title: Automate Failure Simulations
steps.description: Move beyond basic tests by forcing Claude to write edge-case scenarios that usually break production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Perform a stress test analysis on the auth service. Write test cases that specifically simulate expired tokens and malformed JSON payloads.
steps.claude_code_command: claude "add edge case tests for auth service focusing on error handling and boundary conditions"

steps.title: Continuous Integration Loop
steps.description: Bind Claude Code to your pre-commit hooks to ensure no code reaches the client without being vetted.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a shell script that triggers Claude Code to verify code changes before every local git commit.
steps.claude_code_command: claude "create a pre-commit script that runs tests for staged files and requires manual sign-off on failures"

pro_tips: Always use the '--mode=silent' flag in production environments to prevent Claude from being too chatty in your logs.
Use Antigravity's 'semantic-diff' feature to ensure Claude only writes tests for the specific functions you actually modified.
Create a 'testing-persona.md' file in your repo; Claude will read this and adopt a more rigorous testing mindset.
When tests fail, paste the stack trace directly into Claude Code using the '@file' reference for instant root-cause analysis.
Periodically prune your 'test-cache' folder to ensure Antigravity isn't suggesting tests based on deprecated function signatures.
content.next_steps: Integrate these test suites into your CI/CD pipeline (GitHub Actions) to ensure that every push is automatically validated, giving you total peace of mind.
content.conversion_block: Stop manually checking your work and start shipping with absolute confidence. Our community of elite freelancers is already automating their testing and doubling their output. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
